landing.title: Mixpanel: AI Digital Analytics Platform for Product Teams
landing.text:
Mixpanel: AI Digital Analytics Platform for Product Teams
Mixpanel AI
Introducing your always-on product intelligence system.
Learn More
Platform
Solutions
Resources
Pricing

Give product, engineering, and growth teams an edge with proactive AI that knows your data — and what to build next.
Understand user behavior
Watch users in action
Test & ship experiments
Measure what matters
Unify your data stack
Book a Demo
Get Started Free
Product Analytics
Learn what your users actually do. Track events, build funnels, analyze retention, and segment any way you want. Mixpanel AI surfaces the "why" behind the numbers before you even think to ask.
Explore Product Analytics
Web Analytics
Go beyond pageviews to understand real user behavior on your site. Ask questions in plain language and get answers your whole team can act on.
Explore Web Analytics
Session Replay & Heatmaps
Watch the sessions behind your numbers.
Replays are tied directly to your analytics, so
you can go from "drop-off at step 3" to watching
it happen in seconds.
Explore Session Replay
Experiments & Feature Flags
Ship with confidence, not crossed fingers. A/B test against real product metrics and manage feature flags from the same place. No more disconnected tools or inconclusive results.
Explore Experiments
Metric Trees
Build a living map of your KPIs and the drivers beneath them. Map how your key metrics relate to each other, so every team knows which levers to pull, and whether pulling them is actually working.
Explore Metric Trees
Data Warehouse Connectors
Your warehouse data, finally in your hands. Sync from Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, and more. Analyze all of your unified product and backend data in perfect sync.
Explore Warehouse Connectors
Mixpanel AI
Always-on product intelligence for AI-native teams
Mixpanel AI proactively surfaces insights, diagnoses problems, and recommends what to do next, so your team can build with confidence.
Explore Mixpanel AI
Why  teams choose Mixpanel
We’re built to give Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Data teams the confidence to stop guessing and start growing.
Book a Demo
Built for speed
Explore user behavior, conversion trends, and retention patterns in seconds—no data team required.
AI where it helps most
Mixpanel applies AI thoughtfully across the platform to reduce manual work and speed up understanding, always grounded in your data and guided by human judgment.
Flexible by design
Connect your tech stack — from BigQuery to Segment to reverse ETL tools — so your data lives where you need it.
Built to evolve
Whether you're scaling fast or maturing your analytics  practice, Mixpanel grows with your needs.
Enterprise-ready.  Without the complexity.
Mixpanel supports the performance, flexibility, and  governance modern teams need — with none of the legacy bloat.
Explore Mixpanel for Enterprise
Performance that scales
Sub-second query times, even at billions of events per month.
An open ecosystem
Mixpanel plays well with everyone. Connect to your existing stack with flexiblity and no vendor lock-in.
Secure by default
SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, HIPAA-ready, SSO/SAML, and audit logs.
Governance made easy
Define source-of-truth metrics, manage access, and keep teams aligned with trusted data.
“Mixpanel helps us enable our customers for success. What started as a need for simple marketing metrics turned into custom product metrics for more than  100 different stakeholders across the company.”
Drew Ashlock
Senior Product Manager

Edit this page
Scroll to top
Docs
What is Mixpanel?
What is Mixpanel?
Mixpanel will help you better understand your customers and answer questions about your product.
It enables you to track how users engage with your product and analyze this data with interactive reports
that let you query and visualize the results with just a few clicks.
Mixpanel is built on three key concepts:
Events
,
Users
, and
Properties
.
Concepts
Before you get started, you should know three Mixpanel concepts:
Events
are actions that happen in your product
Users
are the people who use your product
Properties
are the attributes of your users and events
Events
An event is a data point that represents an interaction between a user and your product. Events can be a wide range of interactions.
Imagine you run a cafe where customers can purchase a coffee via an app. Each purchase is an event that can be tracked in Mixpanel.
Users
On the other side of an event is a user — the specific individual who completed an interaction with your product.
Each user has a unique identifier that you can use to track their activity. This identifier can be an email address, a username, or a unique ID. Mixpanel uses a unique ID to identify users.
Properties
You can track additional information about
users
and
events
. These details are called
properties
.
An
Event Property
describes an event. For a coffee purchase, the event would be
Purchased Item
and the event properties could be
type
(in this case a Coffee) and
price
(in this case $2.50).
A
User Property
describes a User. This could be their name, email, age, etc.
Properties allow you to create groups of users (aka
cohorts
) and also enable you to filter for certain events or users. These
powerful features make it easy to identify trends and new customer insights.
Next Steps
Now that you understand the basics,
we recommend planning the first events you would like to track
. This will help you understand what you need to get started. Choosing these events should take no more than 5 minutes.
Plan Your Tracked Events
Already Know What You Want to Track?
If you already know the events you want to track, you can skip planning and
start by installing Mixpanel.
Install Mixpanel
What to Track
Was this page useful?
Yes
Could be better

analysis.name: Mixpanel
analysis.tagline: AI Digital Analytics Platform for Product Teams
analysis.value_proposition: Mixpanel gives product, engineering, and growth teams an AI-powered analytics platform that surfaces behavioral insights, runs experiments, and proactively recommends what to build next.
analysis.ideal_customer: Product and growth teams at digital-native companies who need deep behavioral analytics beyond pageviews, with data engineering teams that want warehouse-native flexibility

features.name: product analytics
features.description: Track events, build funnels, analyze retention, and segment users to understand actual product behavior
features.evidence_quote: Track events, build funnels, analyze retention, and segment any way you want.

features.name: web analytics
features.description: Beyond-pageview website behavior analysis with natural language querying
features.evidence_quote: Go beyond pageviews to understand real user behavior on your site.

features.name: mobile analytics
features.description: Analyze and refine mobile app behavior across iOS and Android

features.name: session replay
features.description: Watch recorded user sessions tied directly to analytics events and funnel drop-offs
features.evidence_quote: go from 'drop-off at step 3' to watching it happen in seconds

features.name: heatmaps
features.description: Visual click and scroll maps of user interactions on pages
features.evidence_quote: Session Replay & Heatmaps — Watch the sessions behind your numbers.

features.name: funnels
features.description: Multi-step conversion funnel analysis with drop-off identification

features.name: retention analysis
features.description: Track how users return to product over time with cohort-based retention curves

features.name: flows
features.description: User path analysis showing navigation sequences through a product

features.name: insights reports
features.description: Flexible event-based analysis for arbitrary metric breakdowns and trends

features.name: a/b experiments
features.description: A/B test features against real product metrics with statistical significance reporting
features.evidence_quote: A/B test against real product metrics and manage feature flags from the same place.

features.name: feature flags
features.description: Manage feature rollouts and toggles from within the analytics platform

features.name: metric trees
features.description: Visual KPI map showing how key metrics relate to drivers and sub-metrics
features.evidence_quote: Build a living map of your KPIs and the drivers beneath them.

features.name: data warehouse connectors
features.description: Sync data from Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, and other warehouses into Mixpanel
features.evidence_quote: Sync from Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, and more.

features.name: mixpanel ai
features.description: Always-on AI layer that proactively surfaces insights, diagnoses problems, and recommends actions
features.evidence_quote: Mixpanel AI proactively surfaces insights, diagnoses problems, and recommends what to do next

features.name: mixpanel agent
features.description: Conversational chat interface for querying product data in natural language
features.evidence_quote: Mixpanel Agent — Chat with your data

features.name: headless analytics
features.description: API-only analytics interface for building custom data products without the Mixpanel UI

features.name: mcp server
features.description: Model Context Protocol server enabling AI agents to query Mixpanel data

features.name: agentic automations
features.description: AI-driven automated workflows that act on analytics findings

features.name: spark ai query builder
features.description: Natural language AI query builder for analytics with tiered monthly query limits
features.evidence_quote: Spark AI query builder — 30 queries / month [Free], 60 queries / month [Growth]

features.name: anomaly detection
features.description: Automated detection of unusual spikes or drops in tracked metrics

features.name: root cause analysis
features.description: AI-driven diagnosis of why anomalies or metric changes occurred

features.name: behavioral cohorts
features.description: Segment users by behavioral patterns such as actions taken or not taken

features.name: multi-touch attribution
features.description: Marketing attribution model assigning credit across multiple touchpoints

features.name: custom events
features.description: Virtual events created by combining or transforming raw tracked events

features.name: custom properties
features.description: User-defined derived properties built from existing event or user data

features.name: computed properties
features.description: Formula-based properties calculated dynamically from raw data

features.name: borrowed properties
features.description: Use properties from one event type in analysis of another

features.name: saved metrics & behaviors
features.description: Reusable metric and behavioral definitions shared across reports

features.name: revenue analytics
features.description: Dedicated reporting for tracking revenue events and monetization metrics

features.name: signal correlation analysis
features.description: Identifies correlations between user behaviors and target metric outcomes

features.name: impact analysis
features.description: Measure causal impact of product changes on downstream metrics

features.name: jql
features.description: JavaScript query language for fully custom analytical queries against raw event data

features.name: account-level behavioral analytics

features.evidence_quote: first year is free — founded less than 5 years ago, with up to $8M in total funding

differentiators: Session replays are tied directly to analytics events — navigate from funnel drop-off to watching the session in one click
Always-on AI product intelligence that proactively surfaces insights rather than requiring manual querying
Sub-second query times even at billions of events per month
Metric Trees provide a living strategic map connecting KPIs to operational drivers
Warehouse-native: sync directly from Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift without re-instrumentation
Headless analytics API enables building custom data products without the Mixpanel UI
MCP server integration for AI agent access to analytics data
Open ecosystem with no vendor lock-in across 50+ integrations
Enterprise-grade governance (HIPAA, SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001) without legacy complexity

praised: Deep product analytics capabilities beyond what Google Analytics offers
People Analytics feature praised as a meaningful step forward for understanding users
Flow/path analysis feature well-received on HN launch
complaints: Cost at scale: 'How we saved money by replacing Mixpanel with BigQuery and K8S' (191↑ HN)
Past security breach surfaced as a major concern in HN community
Enough cost/lock-in frustration to fuel open-source alternatives: 'Openpanel – An open-source alternative to Mixpanel' (244↑ HN)

free_tier.limits: 1M events/month, 10K session replays/month, 5 saved reports per seat, 30 Spark AI queries/month

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Core funnels & retention, up to 1M events/mo, 10K session replays/mo, self-serve analytics

tiers.name: Growth
tiers.price_flat_per_month: $0+ (first 1M events free, then $0.28 per 1K events; volume discounts available)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited reports, cohorts, multi-touch attribution, up to 20M events/mo, 20K–500K replays/mo

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Up to 1T events/mo, anomaly detection, root cause analysis, feature flags, SSO, HIPAA, dedicated support

pricing.notes: Growth plan is purely usage-based on events (not seats); 1M events always free regardless of tier. Session replays are a separate add-on volume within Growth (20K free, purchasable up to 500K). Several Enterprise features (feature flags, metric trees, experiment reporting, account-level analytics, data pipelines) are sold as add-ons on Growth. Startup program offers first year free for companies <5 years old with <$8M total funding.
